mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals a rapid labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.5%, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and consumer credit indicate a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns more than 55% of the time, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rising housing starts act as a leading indicator of sustained consumer spending, which supports corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to produce positive 20-day forward returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 240, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 in the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity that directly supports corporate earnings growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS months consistently coincide with negative 5-day SPX returns, or the rolling Spearman correlation between payroll growth and forward returns remains below 0.05 for three consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0%, borrowing costs become restrictive enough to suppress business investment and consumer durable purchases, causing equity valuations to contract over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in >50% of observations where FEDFUNDS stays above 3.0%, or the signal exhibits no predictive power in a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising real estate activity and expanding employment indicates a broad-based economic acceleration that boosts aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint rise in HOUST and PAYEMS fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 45% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain normalization phases, a sustained increase in industrial production relative to historical trends signals improved corporate throughput and margin expansion, generating positive equity returns over a one-month window.
failure_condition: INDPRO values above 104.0 do not produce positive 20-day forward returns more than 55% of the time, or the signal's predictive accuracy degrades below chance levels in a rolling 2-year window.
